location: The collapsed entrance of the Ashen Barrow
scene: Torchlight gutters against damp stone. A goblin sentry, Snik, bars the only passage deeper, blade already drawn. Slumped in the rubble lies an earlier scout, long dead, a water-stained journal still clutched in one hand — its final legible entry warns that the iron door in the deep chamber yields to no key, only to a spoken word: 'ashfall.' Your party: Brother Aldric, a road-worn cleric, and Wisp, a half-elf mage whose spellbook is nearly spent.

barrow_entrance.location: The collapsed entrance of the Ashen Barrow
barrow_entrance.scene: Torchlight gutters against damp stone. A goblin sentry, Snik, bars the only passage deeper, blade already drawn. Slumped in the rubble lies an earlier scout, long dead, a water-stained journal still clutched in one hand — its final legible entry warns that the iron door in the deep chamber yields to no key, only to a spoken word: 'ashfall.' Your party: Brother Aldric, a road-worn cleric, and Wisp, a half-elf mage whose spellbook is nearly spent.

ember_chamber.location: The Ashen Barrow — The Ember Chamber
ember_chamber.scene: The tunnel opens into a low, wide chamber where a stone brazier burns with a sourceless orange flame, throwing restless shadows that make the walls seem to breathe. Two goblins crouch by the far wall, whetstones rasping against notched blades, muttering to one another in low gutturals — they haven't looked up yet. Crude glyphs are gouged into the stone above a heavy door, banded with iron, set in the far wall. Then the scraping stops.

quest_flags.journal_clue: The journal belongs to a grave-robber named Edric who entered the barrow three weeks ago seeking the ember_chamber, where he believed a bound fire-spirit guards a cache of valuables. His final entry warns that Snik — a goblin he hired as a guide — betrayed him and sealed the entrance.

log: [turn 1] flag journal_clue = 'The journal belongs to a grave-robber named Edric who entered the barrow three weeks ago seeking the ember_chamber, where he believed a bound fire-spirit guards a cache of valuables. His final entry warns that Snik — a goblin he hired as a guide — betrayed him and sealed the entrance.'

transcript.text: Aldric pages through the battered journal with a scholar's practiced eye, his brow furrowing deeper with every entry until he reaches the last — a desperate, rain-smeared scrawl. He reads aloud in a low voice: *"Snik has turned on me… he sealed the entrance."* Wisp glances from the page to the goblin lurking at the edge of the torchlight, her fingers drifting instinctively toward her dagger.

narrative.text: Aldric pages through the battered journal with a scholar's practiced eye, his brow furrowing deeper with every entry until he reaches the last — a desperate, rain-smeared scrawl. He reads aloud in a low voice: *"Snik has turned on me… he sealed the entrance."* Wisp glances from the page to the goblin lurking at the edge of the torchlight, her fingers drifting instinctively toward her dagger.
